text.title: LLM Cost Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules that every LLM cost plan must satisfy before any router or cache flips. Validator enforces each mechanically.

rule.statement: A per-stage cost dashboard MUST exist and have ≥7 days of data before any router / cache / batch change is committed.

rationale.source: finops.org foundation; Langfuse / Helicone cost-attribution guides 2026

rule.rationale: Optimization without attribution moves random levers; teams routinely "save 50%" on a stage that was 3% of spend.

rule.statement: Multi-tier routers run a Haiku-class classifier (≤$0.30/1k requests) and dispatch to {haiku, sonnet, opus} by complexity. Total router overhead must be ≤5% of model-saved cost.
rule.rationale: At Haiku $1/$5/MTok vs Opus $5/$25/MTok, routing 80% of traffic away from Opus saves 60-70% on input alone; classifier overhead is rounding error if Haiku-grade.

rule.statement: cache_control attaches only to prefixes ≥1024 tokens (Sonnet/Opus) or ≥2048 (Haiku) that are byte-identical across ≥12 requests/hour. Profile hit-rate ≥50% within 24h or remove the marker.
rule.rationale: Cache write is 1.25-2× base input rate. Below the 12/h threshold or with sub-50% hit rate the marker INCREASES effective cost.

rule.statement: Any workload with ≥100 requests where no human waits on individual completion within 1h MUST route through Batch API (50% discount). Online interactive traffic MUST NOT.

rationale.source: Anthropic Message Batches API; OpenAI Batch API 2026

rule.rationale: 50% off compounds with caching; an offline summarizer with cached corpus + batch can run at ~5% of base rate.

rule.statement: The cost dashboard MUST display output_tokens × output_rate vs input_tokens × input_rate per stage. When output share exceeds 60% of stage cost, routing alone cannot help — escalate to prompt redesign instead.

rationale.source: OpenAI / Anthropic pricing 2026 — output tokens are 3-5× input

rule.rationale: Output bloat (verbose CoT, repeated headers, JSON pretty-printing) is invisible without per-direction attribution; misdiagnosis sends teams down the routing rabbit hole when only prompt edits help.

rule.statement: Every gateway call goes through a budget guard that records cost and breaks with BudgetExceededError at the daily soft cap, alerts at 80% of the cap, and refuses requests at the hard cap.

rationale.source: finops.org runaway-cost patterns; Anthropic spend-cap docs

rule.rationale: Without a hard guard, agentic runaways and infinite tool loops can spend a week of budget in minutes; soft cap is a circuit breaker, hard cap is the brake.
